text.title: Saga Pattern for Distributed Transactions Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real saga implementations.

antipattern.symptom: Order is cancelled but card not refunded; customer escalates; manual reconciliation required weekly.
antipattern.root-cause: Step 3 failed mid-saga; compensations 1+2 not triggered.
antipattern.fix: Add reverse-order compensation policy in orchestrator; ensure each step's compensation is idempotent + retry-safe.

antipattern.symptom: DB shows order created; downstream service never received OrderCreated event; customer sees stuck order.
antipattern.root-cause: Service does `db.save(order); kafka.publish(event)` without outbox; kafka publish failed silently after DB commit.
antipattern.fix: Insert event into transactional outbox table in same DB transaction; Debezium CDC publishes to Kafka asynchronously.

antipattern.symptom: Refund processed twice; customer credited twice; finance team alerts.
antipattern.root-cause: Compensation retries on transient failure; not idempotent.
antipattern.fix: Add idempotency_key=(saga_id, step_id) on each compensation call; downstream dedups.

antipattern.symptom: Adding 5th service to choreography saga: nobody knows the full sequence; latency mystery.
antipattern.root-cause: Choreography hides the saga; flow is implicit in event subscriptions.
antipattern.fix: If saga has >4 steps OR business analyst needs to trace the flow, switch to orchestration (Temporal/Camunda) — saga becomes a visible state machine.
